message:
Add schema contract E2E tests and close bd-vvmd.6.8

Implement comprehensive JSON/log schema contract test suite for
reliability outputs (bd-vvmd.6.8), covering all acceptance criteria:

- API response envelope conformance (success/error) with required
  fields: api_version, timestamp, success, data/error, command
- Error payload structure validation across all categories including
  code, category, remediation, and context fields for path-deps,
  convergence, storage, and process-triage failures
- Hook protocol output variant stability (proceed/abort/retry/skip)
- Reliability event schema versioning with backward/forward
  compatibility checks against RELIABILITY_EVENT_SCHEMA_VERSION
- Error catalog completeness and category range invariants ensuring
  every ErrorCode maps to a valid ErrorCategory
- Machine parser compatibility for emitted output artifacts via
  round-trip serialization and golden schema validation
- Legacy error code migration fidelity verifying bidirectional
  mapping between legacy codes and modern ErrorCode values

The 951-line test file (rch-common/tests/schema_contract_e2e.rs)
provides the end-to-end contract verification layer that protects
operator and automation integrations from schema drift.

Also integrates the new schema_contract test family into the unified
reliability E2E suite (tests/e2e/unified_reliability_suite.sh) so it
runs as part of standard and nightly CI tiers.

Updates the beads issue tracker to mark bd-vvmd.6.8 as closed/done.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-vvmd.6.5","title":"Codify SLO guardrails and regression checks for reliability features","description":"Define measurable guardrails (hook latency, convergence latency, failure-rate budgets) and integrate checks into validation flow. Ensure regressions are detected before release.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Guardrails define measurable budgets for hook latency, convergence latency, fallback rate, and triage overhead.
- Regression checks run in CI/nightly and fail clearly when thresholds are exceeded.
- SLO definitions include measurement windows and error-budget consumption semantics.
- Alert criteria avoid noise through debouncing/hysteresis where appropriate.
- Reports identify top regressions with actionable attribution data.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:09.024659545Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:52.022416760Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["observability","quality","rch-rel-2026q1","slo"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1qhj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:50.766352173Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1yt6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:50.972945701Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:50.558134995Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:22.274463671Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:22.472492504Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:36.665207807Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":199,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SLO expansion: include cancellation-path reliability/latency/fallback budgets and UX diagnosability signals from bd-1yt6 and bd-1qhj.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.6.6","title":"Add performance budget benchmarks for new reliability pipeline","description":"Implement benchmark and regression checks for preflight/convergence/triage/ballast flows with explicit user-facing performance budgets. Include both microbenchmarks and scenario-level benchmarks using reliability E2E suites, fault-injection runs, and long-duration soak results.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Hook decision latency budgets are benchmarked with threshold enforcement.
- Worker-selection + preflight/convergence latency budgets are benchmarked under normal and degraded conditions.
- End-to-end overhead benchmarks include full reliability suite, fault-injection, and soak paths.
- Benchmark output includes clear regression deltas and top contributors for triage.
- CI/nightly gate fails on budget regressions unless explicit override policy is used.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:25.247230478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:51.175215362Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["budgets","observability","performance","rch-rel-2026q1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1yt6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:51.174973419Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:29.242386090Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:19.582196787Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:19.793331867Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":138,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Rationale: new reliability logic adds control-plane overhead; explicit benchmark gates protect the core RCH latency budgets and prevent regressions.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.6.7","title":"Implement feature flags + staged rollout/canary plan for reliability features","description":"Add configuration flags for each major reliability subsystem (path closure sync, repo convergence gate, storage ballast, process triage) and define staged rollout/canary process. Include rapid-disable controls, health gates, and rollback criteria to de-risk production adoption.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Feature flags exist per subsystem (path closure, convergence gate, ballast policy, process triage).
- Canary plan defines enablement sequence, health gates, and auto-disable triggers.
- Rollback procedure can disable new reliability features without service disruption.
- Flag states and rollout decisions are observable and audit-logged.
- Tests validate mixed-flag compatibility and safe fallback behavior under partial rollout.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:25.452621514Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:05:10.947893918Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["observability","operations","rch-rel-2026q1","rollout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:04:50.352378189Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:29.642291179Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:29.443917701Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:36.868874965Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":139,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Rationale: staged rollout with kill switches is required to de-risk deployment across heterogeneous workers and to contain blast radius during early adoption.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:32Z"},{"id":185,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Rollout expansion: canary health gates must include helper-contract compatibility status (bd-vvmd.6.11) before broad enablement.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:05:10Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-vvmd.6.8","title":"Add JSON/log schema contract tests for reliability outputs","description":"Define and enforce contract tests for machine-readable outputs introduced by reliability work: JSON envelopes, error-code payloads, status endpoints, and structured test logs. Protect operator and automation integrations from schema drift.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Golden tests verify JSON envelope conformance for success/error responses across hook/machine/CLI flows.
- Error payload tests verify code/category/remediation/context fields for path-deps, convergence, storage, and process-triage failures.
- Structured log schema is versioned and validated in tests with backward/forward compatibility rules explicitly documented.
- CI fails on schema drift unless schema-version bump + migration notes are included.
- At least one end-to-end test validates machine parser compatibility against emitted output artifacts.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:32:28.054952471Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T20:20:59.902519754Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","logging","observability","rch-rel-2026q1","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:31.378835928Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:31.592173623Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:31.800138637Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:32.013664053Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":157,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Contract-level schema testing is critical for user trust and automation stability. This bead makes output compatibility a first-class release requirement, not a best-effort side effect.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:34:48Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-vvmd.6.9","title":"Add reliability doctor command with actionable remediation guidance","description":"Implement a dedicated operator-focused reliability doctor surface (`rch doctor --reliability`) that summarizes readiness across path-deps, convergence drift, disk pressure, process debt, helper-contract compatibility, and rollout/canary posture, with concrete repair commands and dry-run modes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Doctor output includes machine JSON and human-readable remediation modes.
- Diagnostics cover canonical topology, required repo presence/freshness, disk-pressure risk, process-triage debt, helper compatibility status, rollout flag posture, and output-schema compatibility.
- Every failure mode includes explicit remediation command(s) and expected validation check.
- Dry-run mode is non-destructive and suitable for CI/preflight gating.
- Unit/integration tests validate stable output schema and deterministic decisioning.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:39:45.508394494Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:49.115165456Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doctor","observability","operations","rch-rel-2026q1","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1yt6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:10:49.115037967Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.9","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:16.582509976Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.9","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:04:50.158131149Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.9","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:16.794336431Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.9","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:07:31.555151786Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.9","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.6.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:17.006812753Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":164,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"User-outcome rationale: consolidated reliability doctor output shortens time-to-repair by giving deterministic preflight checks and concrete fix commands in both human and machine formats.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:37Z"},{"id":181,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Doctor scope expanded by dependency: include explicit helper-contract compatibility diagnostics from bd-vvmd.6.11 in both machine JSON and human remediation output.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:04:50Z"},{"id":187,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.6.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Optimization revision: doctor now models helper compatibility and rollout posture in addition to core runtime health, improving preflight confidence and reducing surprise regressions during staged enablement.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:07:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.7","title":"Workstream: Comprehensive Unit/Integration/E2E Validation with Structured Logs","description":"Purpose: provide confidence that reliability features work under realistic multi-repo, multi-worker conditions. Expand fixtures, scripts, and assertions to cover local path dependencies, repo convergence, disk-pressure recovery, and stuck-process remediation. Logging requirements: detailed machine-readable phase logs to support fast root-cause analysis when failures occur.

## Success Criteria
- Shared E2E harness supports deterministic scenario execution and artifact capture.
- Scenario suites cover happy-path, degraded-path, fault-injection, and long-duration soak conditions.
- Reliability coverage matrix maps every requirement/failure mode to executable tests and log assertions.
- CI tiers (smoke/nightly/full) enforce reproducible execution and regression visibility.
- Release gate closes only after comprehensive reliability evidence is complete.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:01.423639215Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:34:37.344873813Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","quality","rch","rch-rel-2026q1","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:23.681857677Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:24:23.507598611Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":135,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Quality bar: every major scenario must be represented by deterministic fixtures and phase-logged E2E tests. Logs must allow postmortem reconstruction without rerunning tests.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:20:22Z"},{"id":147,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Validation scope: tests must prove both correctness and controlled degradation. Matrix includes happy path, missing repos, stale repos, partial sync, network faults, disk pressure, stuck process overlap, and daemon restart races. Required artifacting: per-phase machine-readable logs plus retained diagnostics for postmortem replay. Release should not proceed on partial coverage.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:28:16Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.7.1","title":"Create deterministic multi-repo fixtures under /data/projects (+ /dp symlink cases)","description":"Build reusable fixtures representing realistic cross-repo Cargo path-dependency graphs, including symlink variants and failure fixtures. Fixtures must support unit/integration/e2e layers and produce reproducible outcomes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Fixture set includes representative multi-repo Cargo path-dependency graphs and failure variants.
- Fixtures cover canonical `/data/projects` and `/dp` symlink alias forms with deterministic reset behavior.
- Each fixture has metadata describing expected readiness, failure mode, and assertion targets.
- Fixtures are reusable across unit, integration, fault-injection, and soak suites.
- Fixture generation/reset scripts produce reproducible outcomes across repeated runs.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:09.233159863Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T18:30:58.989687675Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T18:30:58.989668670Z","close_reason":"Completed deterministic multi-repo fixture system with metadata, alias invariants, reset semantics, tests, and logged e2e script; see comment #208.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fixtures","path-deps","rch-rel-2026q1","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:17.606501703Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":208,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.7.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# bd-vvmd.7.1 completion artifact: deterministic multi-repo fixture system

## Scope
Implemented a reusable deterministic fixture system for cross-repo Cargo path-dependency scenarios, rooted in canonical `/data/projects` topology with `/dp` alias expectations, including failure fixtures, explicit metadata, and reproducible reset behavior.

## Code changes

### 1) Deterministic multi-repo fixture API (`rch-common/src/e2e/fixtures.rs`)
Added a new fixture subsystem with explicit topology/configuration and scenario metadata:

- Topology constants:
  - `DEFAULT_MULTI_REPO_CANONICAL_ROOT = "/data/projects"`
  - `DEFAULT_MULTI_REPO_ALIAS_ROOT = "/dp"`
  - `DEFAULT_MULTI_REPO_FIXTURE_NAMESPACE = "rch_multi_repo_path_deps"`
- Config and lifecycle:
  - `MultiRepoFixtureConfig`
  - `reset_multi_repo_fixtures(&MultiRepoFixtureConfig)`
  - `reset_default_multi_repo_fixtures()`
- Structured metadata and result model:
  - `MultiRepoFixtureSet`
  - `MultiRepoFixtureMetadata`
  - `FixtureReadiness`
  - `FixtureFailureMode`
  - `FixtureLayer`
- Explicit error model:
  - `MultiRepoFixtureError`
  - `MultiRepoFixtureResult<T>`

### 2) Topology and safety invariants
Fixture generation now enforces:

- canonical root and alias root must be absolute
- alias root must be a symlink
- alias symlink target must resolve exactly to canonical root
- namespace reset is deterministic (`remove + recreate` under namespace only)
- manifest snapshot (`fixture_manifest.json`) is produced for deterministic introspection

### 3) Scenario coverage (representative graphs + failure variants)
Implemented these reusable scenarios:

1. `ready_relative_transitive`
- three-repo transitive relative-path graph (`app -> util -> core`)
- expected ready

2. `ready_alias_absolute`
- absolute alias-path dependency via `/dp/...`
- expected ready

3. `fail_missing_path_dep`
- missing local path dependency
- expected failure (`MissingPathDependency`)

4. `fail_outside_canonical_dep`
- absolute dependency outside canonical root
- expected failure (`OutsideCanonicalRootDependency`)

5. `fail_invalid_manifest`
- malformed Cargo.toml
- expected failure (`InvalidCargoManifest`)

Each scenario includes assertion targets and reusable layer tags (`unit`, `integration`, `fault_injection`, `soak`).

### 4) Re-exports for cross-suite reuse (`rch-common/src/e2e/mod.rs`)
Added public re-exports so unit/integration/e2e/fault/soak suites can consume the same fixture API and metadata contract.

### 5) Logged E2E script (`tests/e2e/path_dependency_fixtures.sh`)
Added a dedicated script that:

- validates real host invariants (`/data/projects` exists, `/dp` symlink resolves correctly)
- runs deterministic fixture tests with clear step logging
- exits non-zero on invariant/test failure

## Validation evidence

Commands run:

- `cargo fmt --check` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ❌ (pre-existing workspace blocker in `rch/src/commands/daemon.rs:627`, `clippy::items_after_test_module`)
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p rch-common multi_repo_fixture_ -- --nocapture` ✅ (4/4 new fixture tests passed)
- `tests/e2e/path_dependency_fixtures.sh` ✅ (topology checks + fixture test execution passed)

## Acceptance criteria mapping

- Representative multi-repo Cargo path-dependency graphs + failure variants: ✅
- Canonical `/data/projects` + `/dp` alias coverage with deterministic reset: ✅
- Per-fixture metadata with readiness/failure/assertion targets: ✅
- Reusable across unit/integration/fault-injection/soak layers: ✅
- Reproducible generation/reset via code path + dedicated logged script: ✅

## Notes for downstream beads

- This directly unblocks reliability harness and path-dependency E2E expansion beads by providing a single deterministic fixture substrate.
- Downstream suites should consume `MultiRepoFixtureSet` metadata instead of duplicating fixture assumptions.
","created_at":"2026-02-16T18:30:55Z"}]}

@@ -230,6 +230,18 @@ run_classification_regression() {
   )
 }
 
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Family: schema contract (bd-vvmd.6.8)
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+run_schema_contract() {
+  log "running JSON/log schema contract tests"
+  (
+    cd "$PROJECT_ROOT"
+    CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target cargo test -p rch-common --test schema_contract_e2e -- --nocapture
+  )
+}
+
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 # Nightly-only families
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@@ -314,6 +326,7 @@ main() {
   run_family "topology_fixtures"      run_topology_fixtures
   run_family "fault_injection"           run_fault_injection
   run_family "classification_regression" run_classification_regression
+  run_family "schema_contract"           run_schema_contract
 
   # --- Nightly families (only in nightly/full mode) ---
   case "$E2E_MODE" in
